rulings.Case Title: SANDRA PEDROIA VS. SPECTRUM JUNGLE LAB, CORP. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Reconsideration Of Order Granting Judgment On The Pleadings
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Friday, March 7, 2014, Line 1, PLAINTIFF SANDRA PEDROIA'S Motion For Reconsideration Of Order Granting Judgment On The Pleadings. The motion is denied. The Court lacks the power to grant reconsideration because judgment was entered on February 20, 2014. See Ramon v. Aerospace Corp. (1996) 50 Cal.App.4th 1233, 1236; APRI Ins. Co. v. Sup. Crt. (1999) 76 Cal.App.4th 176, 181-182. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: LISA HABBESHAW VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Independent Medical Examination
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Friday, March 7, 2014, Line 8, DEFENDANT HONORWAY INVESTMENT CORPORATION's Motion To Compel Independent Medical Examination. Defendant's motion to compel physical examination granted. Notwithstanding the parties' modified/agreed Notice of Examination terms, defendant's examiner, Dr. Cassini, insisted on audiotaping his examination simultaneously with plaintiff's counsel's same effort. That disputed issue is not significant in the context of the overall formal medical-examination proceeding. Plaintiff's allegations of injury are complex and her medical treatment is reflected in many thousands of pages of entries. Under the circumstances, to deny the medical examination would exalt process over substance. The defendant would be denied the right to obtain medical evidence refuting plaintiff's claims or agreeing with them. Had the audiotaping been allowed there would be no conceivable prejudice to the plaintiff. If the defendant chooses to continue with Dr. Cassini as its examiner, simultaneous audiotaping shall be permitted. The court will not provide a court reporter for this discovery hearing. If any party wishes a reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

The parties shall meet and confer on the appointment of a referee prior to the hearing on March 7, 2014. rulings.Case Title: BANK OF THE WEST VS. BODY DETAILS, INC. A CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION For Leave To File First Amended Answer And Affirmative Defenses
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Friday, March 7, 2014, Line 12, DEFENDANTs BODY DETAILS, INC. A CORPORATION and ROBERT OZAKI's MOTION For Leave To File First Amended Answer And Affirmative Defenses. Denied. The motion does not conform to California Rule of Court 3.1324, which provides that a declaration must accompany the motion. Although Defendant submits such a declaration in reply, the Court finds that it would be unduly prejudicial to Plaintiff to consider this evidence, given that Plaintiff does not have an opportunity to respond. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: BANK OF THE WEST VS. BODY DETAILS, INC. A CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff Bank Of The West'S Motion For Summary Judgment Or Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Friday, March 7, 2014, Line 12, Plaintiff Bank Of The West'S Motion For Summary Judgment Or Summary Adjudication. Plaintiff's motion for summary judgment is granted. Plaintiff's request for judicial notice is granted. Plaintiff met its burden in establishing that Defendant Body Details entered into two commercial lease agreements with Plaintiff, (Undisputed Material Fact ("UF" nos. 1, 10), Defendant Robert Hatsuo Ozaki executed guarantees on the agreements (UF nos. 5, 14), Plaintiffs performed under the agreements (UF nos. 2, 11), Defendants breached the two agreements and two guarantees (UF nos. 6, 8, 16, 17), and Plaintiff was damaged as a result of the breaches. (UF nos. 7, 16.) The burden then shifted to Defendants. Even assuming that Defendants are not precluded from raising their affirmative defenses for failing to include them in their answer, Defendants still have not met their burden of proof. Defendants introduce no evidence in support of their theories that Trinity is a distinct legal entity from the Bank of the West or that Trinity was required to or failed to file a fictitious name statement. (See Rochlis v. Walt Disney Co. (1993) 19 Cal.App.4th 201, 219 (disapproved on other grounds), holding that claims and theories not supported by admissible evidence do not raise a triable issue.) Further, the unambiguous language of the contracts state that Trinity is "a division of Bank of the West." (Declaration of James Roach, Exs. 1, 5 - 7.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The proposed order submitted with the moving papers will be signed by the Court upon the granting of this motion. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: JOSEPH CONNELLY VS. COLLEEN HOLLAND et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn To Compel Deposition Of Joseph Connelly And For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Friday, March 7, 2014, Line 1, DEFENDANT COLLEEN HOLLAND'S Motion To Compel Deposition Of Joseph Connelly And For Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Charles Geerhart, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Granted in part. Joseph Connelly is to appear for deposition on a date to be set at the hearing of this motion. The parties are ordered to meet and confer and set a deposition date, said deposition to occur within 30 days of the hearing. The hearing date shall be inserted into the order, which the Court will sign at the hearing. Sanctions are granted in part, as follows: As to Joseph Connelly's late cancellation and non-appearance at the October 15, 2013 deposition, the Court awards $350 representing the non-refundable cost of the court reporter and videographer. The Court also awards $700 in attorney fees, representing two hours of preparation time for that deposition, recognizing that counsel will to some extent have to re-prepare for the subsequent deposition months later. Total sanctions of $1,050 are to be paid by plaintiff Joseph Connelly to defendant Holland's attorneys within 10 days of the date of service of this Order. Sanctions are denied as to the present motion. It is difficult to determine from moving papers what efforts defendant took to re-schedule the deposition after the matter's return from bankruptcy court, and plaintiff's counsel avers that he did offer possible dates for the Connelly deposition. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to chuck@paoligeerhart.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: MERVYN SILBERBERG VS. UNITED SERVICES AUTOMOBILE ASSOCIATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Terminating Sanctions, Or In The Alternative, Dismissal Of Plaintiffs Financial Loss Claims
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Friday, March 7, 2014, Line 3, DEFENDANTS UNITED SERVICES AUTOMOBILE ASSOCIATION AND USAA CASUALTY INSURANCE COMPANY'S And Motion For Terminating Sanctions, Or In The Alternative, Dismissal Of Plaintiff's Financial Loss Claims. Pro Tem Judge Charles Geerhart, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Motion granted in part. The Court finds that plaintiff, in making various claims for financial loss and loss of business opportunities, has placed his financial condition in issue and therefore waived, at least in part, his privacy rights as to his relevant financial documents. Plaintiff alleges he suffered financial losses due to lack of funds, which he alleges was caused by his settlement of the Curran claim. Plaintiff has a relatively complicated array of financial holdings. For USAA to defend itself, it would need all documents necessary for its experts to conduct a complete analysis of plaintiff's financial condition. Defendant's Demands for Production (RPD) Nos. 24 and 25 are aimed at discovering those financial documents supporting plaintiff's case, which would include checking/savings/financial statements, profit and loss statements for the businesses for which he claims he sustained losses, document showing cash flow (income and expenses) for the various rental properties, and proof of lost future business opportunities, such as correspondence, finance/credit applications, proposed contracts, etc. This Court's prior order of 11/4/13 afforded plaintiff one last opportunity to fully respond to RPD 24 and 25. The evidence shows he has not complied with the prior order. Under these circumstances, an evidentiary sanction is appropriate under CCP § 2023.030(c). Therefore, the Court imposes evidence sanctions as follows: The Court orders that plaintiff shall be precluded at trial from claiming any damages for economic loss other than the $500,000 settlement of the Curran case, and attorney's fees and costs. This order does not affect claims for non-economic damages. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to chuck@paoligeerhart.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Friday, March 7, 2014, Line 4, Defendants Bently Holdings California Limited Partnership And Christopher Bently's Motion For Protective Order Re Privileged Communications (PART 1/2 OF TENTATIVE RULING). Pro Tem Judge Charles Geerhart, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Granted. The Court finds that communications (principally emails) between plaintiff Erik Parker and Bently Holdings California Limited Partnership (BHCLP) attorney Michael Donner in connection with the Papaliolios matter fall under the attorney-client privilege. These communications include the various versions of the Erik Parker declaration prepared by counsel, unless they have been made public. Erik Parker had the title Security Manager, and supervised BHCLP security personnel. When attorney Donner communicated with Erik Parker seeking his input for his declaration in the Papaliolios litigation, it was expected in plaintiff Parker's capacity as Security Manager that he was required to make statements on behalf of BHCLP. See D.I. Chadbourne, Inc. v. Sup.Ct. (1964) 60 Cal.2d 723 at 736-739. The same rationale applies to Samuel Calpo, who, although not a manager, was allegedly working security at the event underlying the Papaliolios litigation, and would be required to provide statements to BHCLP as a member of the security staff. Communications (involving the Papaliolios matter) between attorney Donner and Brady Frey of BHCLP, and between attorney Donner and any other employees of BHCLP (such as Anne Hill, Samuel Calpo, and "Vivian") are also protected from discovery under the attorney-client privilege. TENTATIVE RULING CONTINUED. SEE PART 2/2 OF TENTATIVE RULING. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: ERIK PARKER VS. BENTLY HOLDINGS CALIFORNIA LIMITED PARTNERSHIP et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendants Bently Holdings California Limited Partnership And Christopher Bently'S Motion For Protective Order Re Privileged Communications (Part 2/2 Of Tentative Ruling).
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Friday, March 7, 2014, Line 4, Defendants Bently Holdings California Limited Partnership And Christopher Bently's Motion For Protective Order Re Privileged Communications (Part 2/2 Of Tentative Ruling). The Court finds that the crime-fraud exception to the attorney-client privilege does not apply. There is insufficient evidence proffered that BHCLP sought or obtained legal assistance "to enable or aid" it to commit a crime or fraud. Evidence Code sec. 956; Geilim v. Superior Court (1991) 234 Cal.App.3d 166, 174. The Court orders plaintiff and his counsel to return to defense counsel within 10 days of the entry of this Order all copies, including electronic versions, of the above-referenced attorney-client privileged material which plaintiff and/or counsel has in his/her possession, custody or control. The Court also issues a Protective Order precluding plaintiff from seeking in discovery the production or content of attorney-client privileged communications made in the Papaliolios matter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to chuck@paoligeerhart.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)
